name: nicotiana rustica
intensity: Strong; significantly more intense than common tobacco
mechanism: used in shamanic rituals for grounding and spirit communication
effects: used in amazonian rituals for purification and divination
interactions: other nicotinic agonists and mao inhibitors.
May interact dangerously with MAOIs and stimulants
safetyNotes: Caution advised due to high potency,traditional supervision often required,Nausea,vomiting,dizziness,hypertension,High (dose-dependent nicotine exposure),Estimated 30–50 mg nicotine in adults,nausea,tachycardia,dizziness at high doses [1,0].,High risk of nicotine overdose,very toxic.,Toxic – high nicotine,No direct toxicity data. description: A highly potent tobacco species used in South American rituals. Contains much higher nicotine levels than common tobacco and is smoked, snuffed, or brewed in ceremonial settings.
dosage: No direct dosage data. Contextual inference: potent nicotiana rustica tobacco burned in ritual for focus and protection.. Nicotine agonizes nicotinic acetylcholine receptors,contains β,carbolines (harmala alkaloids) acting as reversible MAO inhibitors [1,0,1,9]..
